subject: I don't want to cross my fingers
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bernie 2016
body:
#
In about 48 hours, several million more Americans will have cast their votes for who they want to be their nominee for president.
But I don't want to cross my fingers watching votes come in Tuesday night. **I want to know that our team did everything in our power to help Bernie win before the polls close.**
What we've done together is historic. We've built a movement that is challenging the political, financial, and media establishment - and winning. **Don't stop now.**
[**Help us do everything in our power to win big on Tuesday. Make a $3 contribution to our campaign now.**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/path-to-the-nomination?refcode=em160313pm2-full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/path-to-the-nomination?refcode=em160313pm2-full")
After last week's historic comeback in Michigan, a big win on Tuesday night in Florida, Illinois, Missouri, North Carolina, and Ohio would absolutely stun the establishment. They wouldn't know what to do.
But we can only do this if we do it together. Let's show 'em what we've got.
[**Chip in $3 and help us go all out for Tuesday's big primaries.**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/path-to-the-nomination?refcode=em160313pm2-full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/path-to-the-nomination?refcode=em160313pm2-full")
In solidarity,
Jeff Weaver
Campaign Manager

name: Rand Paul
subject: Hillary said WHAT?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for Senate
body:
Patriot,
Just when I thought she couldn't be anymore
unqualified, Hillary Clinton brags about destroying coal country.

What kind of presidential candidate brags
about putting hard working Americans out of business?

Over the weekend, Hillary Clinton said out
loud what the national democrat party has been trying to do
quietly for the past 8 years.

She vows to finish Barack Obama's war on coal
and Kentucky.

I won't let her do it. I am running to fight
for Kentucky and to stop the democrats from killing more coal
jobs.

Stand with me to end
the war on coal by donating today

The problem with liberals like Hillary and
President Obama, is that they want to abuse the authority of the
executive branch to choose winners and losers in business.
They will regulate a business straight out of
business if it benefits their agenda somehow - and that's what
they're doing to coal.
Through executive order and through the EPA,
our coal industry has faced a major onslaught of regulation that
has burdened the business incredibly.
This has not only been a war against one of
Kentucky's leading industries -- it has literally been a war on
jobs. Ten thousand workers have lost their jobs because of
President Obama's malicious disregard for American workers.
Hillary thinks this is all A-okay? She wants
to continue those plans?
That's incredible. Really, it's madness.
I have seen the look on the faces of hard
working people in this country, who very much want to work, who
are being laid off because of President Obama's war on coal.
I've personally witnessed the harmful effects
of these disastrous, abusive regulations.
That anyone would so proudly pledge to
continue these mistakes is just shocking.
I have fought to stop his war on coal at
every turn, and let me tell you... if Hillary Clinton thinks
she's going to one-up Obama's vendetta against coal, she's got
another thing coming.
I will not tolerate this war on jobs.
I'll fight for Kentucky every day in the
Senate. I will stand strong for coal and for American business.
Will you stand with me?

In liberty,
Rand Paul

Pitch in $50 TODAY >>

Pitch in $75 TODAY >>

Pitch in $100 TODAY
>>

amount >>

body:
Dear Friend,

Over the weekend,
with victories in Wyoming and Guam, the Cruz campaign continues
to coalesce conservatives, Republicans, and Americans who care
about the future of our country.

As we've demonstrated
now time and time again, we are the only campaign that has beaten
Donald Trump repeatedly and has a credible path to the
nomination. That's why we've seen leaders like Carly Fiorina,
Mike Lee, and Mark Levin join our campaign. That's why the
leaders of National Review, William F. Buckley's magazine that
helped propel Ronald Reagan to the presidency, have endorsed
Cruz.

We care about our
kids and grandkids - and this election is about them. If we
continue to unite conservatives across America, we will together
bring back the prosperity and opportunity that has always defined
the American way.

Best,

Alice Stewart
Communications
Director

subject: You’ve never let me down before
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a campaign supported by hundreds of thousands of grassroots donors
body:
I know you won't let me down | Friend --

We’re just one day out from five huge primaries in Ohio, Florida, North Carolina, Missouri, and Illinois. I’m fighting my heart out for every vote, and I’m so grateful you’re fighting just as hard. **[If you’re with me, can you chip in to the March Victory Fund right now to help us win?](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=15&ms=MjQ5NjQ3MTES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=NzYyMjI5NTg2S0&mt=1&rt=0)**

Senator Sanders has proven time and again that he’s going to compete for every vote in this campaign, and his supporters are working hard to help him win. They raised $5 million in a single day after they won Michigan, and they’re going to outspend us in some of these states tomorrow, just as they did in Michigan, New Hampshire, Colorado, and more.

Winning this nomination won’t be easy -- but no matter how tough things get, this team has never once let me down. **I know we can make history together, starting tomorrow and all the way to the White House. I hope you’ll chip in right now to be a part of it:** [ | | [**Donate $1**](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=MjQ5NjQ3MTES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=NzYyMjI5NTg2S0&mt=1&rt=0)

Hillary

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: We are at a moment of truth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bernie 2016
body:
_"I am writing to inform you that I will be a candidate for President of the United States. I ask for your support."_
Sisters and Brothers -
Those were the first words I used to launch our campaign almost 11 months ago. In that same announcement, I also wondered if it may already be too late to stop the billionaire class and the forces of greed from buying the presidency.
But what we have proved since that day is that it is not too late. **Through our campaign, we have started to turn back the assault on the foundations of our democracy and our future.** But tomorrow is probably the single most important day for our campaign since we started.
We've set a goal of reaching 150,000 individual contributions from yesterday morning to midnight tonight. Right now, we're more than halfway there. **So I have to ask directly:**
[**Can I count on you to make a $3 contribution before our midnight deadline? Reaching our goal could be the difference between victory and defeat in the Democratic primary.**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/a-moment-of-truth?refcode=em160314am-full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/a-moment-of-truth?refcode=em160314am-full")
We are now, as we were when we started this campaign, at a moment of truth. We need to face up to the reality of where we are as a nation, and the same old establishment ideas will no longer be enough.
I believe America is ready for a new path to the future. And tomorrow night's results will go a long way toward determining if we'll get that chance. [**That 's why your $3 contribution is so important.**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/a-moment-of-truth?refcode=em160314am-full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/a-moment-of-truth?refcode=em160314am-full")
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders

body:
Courageous Conservatives \- Reigniting the Promise of America
[](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/34164115:WX92PDCNt:m:1:1104269936:5D2545F9AF679760DBDC20C47AB40B22:r)
**Hello Friend,**
There is a simple fact in the race for the Republican nomination for president: only two candidates -- Donald Trump and Ted Cruz -- have a chance to go into the convention in Cleveland, Ohio, for the Republican National Convention with the 1,237 delegates necessary to win the nomination. Following Super Saturday last weekend, the _Washington Post_ [wrote](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/34164116:WX92PDCNt:m:1:1104269936:5D2545F9AF679760DBDC20C47AB40B22:r) the "results show Rubio collapsing, Trump stoppable and Cruz gaining momentum."
The voters are now faced with a simple choice: who do you want to be our Republican nominee and next President? Do you want someone who has, like Donald Trump for decades, been part of the problem in Washington by donating to politicians like Jimmy Carter, Chuck Schumer, Harry Reid, John Kerry, and Hillary Clinton to benefit him and his business dealings? Or do you want your next president to be someone who, like Ted Cruz, has spent a lifetime defending our Constitution and Bill of Rights?
As a result of this simple reality, the theme that permeated throughout the week was unity as we saw conservative leaders across the country unite behind Ted Cruz. We were proud to announce the endorsements of Carly Fiorina, U.S. Sen. Mike Lee, Mark Levin, Erick Erickson, Ben Ferguson, and _National Review_!
[](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/34164117:WX92PDCNt:m:1:1104269936:5D2545F9AF679760DBDC20C47AB40B22:r)
The reason they are all supporting Ted is very simple: he is the only candidate with a credible path to defeating Donald Trump at the ballot box -- not placing all his hopes on a contested convention -- and he has spent his entire life defending our Constitution and Bill of Rights. The stakes of this election are simply too high to take a chance with Donald Trump.
Five more states head to the ballot box this Tuesday: Florida, Illinois, Missouri, North Carolina, and Ohio. If you live in one of those states, [you can find information about your polling place and commit to vote for Ted here](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/34164117:WX92PDCNt:m:1:1104269936:5D2545F9AF679760DBDC20C47AB40B22:r). Then once you've done that, please take the time to reach out to your family, friends, and neighbors and encourage them to do the same. And if you don't live in one of these five states, we still need your help. Odds are you may know some people who do live in Florida, Illinois, Missouri, North Carolina, or Ohio -- please pick up the phone and call them. Explain to them the stakes of this election and encourage them to 1) commit to vote for Ted and 2) commit to bringing 9 other people with them to do the same.
If you know of any people who might need additional convincing before Tuesday, Ted participated in a townhall with Megyn Kelly in Raleigh, North Carolina, that I'd encourage you to [share with everyone you know](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/34164118:WX92PDCNt:m:1:1104269936:5D2545F9AF679760DBDC20C47AB40B22:r).
[](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/34164118:WX92PDCNt:m:1:1104269936:5D2545F9AF679760DBDC20C47AB40B22:r)
So to conclude, I want to ask you a favor. Please reach out to everyone you know -- including those who may be supporting other candidates. And please make the case to them that if they truly believe that Donald Trump is not the right person to take on Hillary Clinton in the fall, that there is only one candidate with a shot to beat him, and that is Ted Cruz. Then please encourage them to join our team at [tedcruz.org/volunteer](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/34164119:WX92PDCNt:m:1:1104269936:5D2545F9AF679760DBDC20C47AB40B22:r).
To victory,
**Jeff Roe**
**Campaign Manager**
**Cruz for President**

name: BernieSanders.com
subject: It's so damn close
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bernie 2016
body:
#
Sisters and Brothers -
Let me tell you, Bernie is going after every. last. vote. today. Before midnight, he'll hold five rallies in four of the states with a primary tomorrow: Ohio, North Carolina, Missouri, and Illinois.
He's working so hard because we are so damn close. This morning, a fresh wave of polls came out showing Bernie leading or within the margin of error in three of those states. And in the fourth, we're well within the margin of some of Michigan's polling in the final days.
Bernie's doing everything he can today, and that's why he's asking you to do the same. He doesn't go around asking millionaires for money - our campaign is funded by emails like this. So we have to ask:
[**Can Bernie count on you to make a $3 contribution before midnight tonight? We 're almost at our goal of 150,000 contributions before our midnight deadline. Put us over the top.**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/a-moment-of-truth?refcode=em160314pm1-full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/a-moment-of-truth?refcode=em160314pm1-full")
After tomorrow night, the map shifts dramatically in our favor. And I mean dramatically. If we can do well tomorrow night, we're going to win. Those are the stakes. And that's why hitting our goal is so important.
In solidarity,
Jeff Weaver
Campaign Manager
Bernie 2016

subject: reaching out directly
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Katie McGinty for Senate

---
Friend -- The past few months have been simply amazing. Our campaign has connected with thousands of voters across Pennsylvania, we've received endorsements from groups as varied as the League of Conservation Voters and SEIU, and we're steaming ahead towards Primary Day.

But these last weeks before the primary are our most important yet. That's why I'm coming to you directly.

**In a race as tight as this one, there 's no room for error.** Every fundraising goal is a must-hit if we want to end Pat Toomey's failed leadership and bring Pennsylvania values to the U.S. Senate.

**[My team tells me we need to raise $10,000 by midnight Wednesday to stay on track to hit our end-of-quarter goal. Friend, can I count on you to pitch in $5 now?](https://act.myngp.com/el/-386059307268964352/-4191984837919244288?refcode=e20160314&refcode2=e20160314nd&amount=25)**

Pennsylvania can't afford six more years of Toomey -- to say nothing of a Republican Senate majority combined with a Donald Trump presidency.

But that nightmare has a real chance of coming true if each and every one of us doesn't give this election our all. **There are still so many undecided voters out there who we need to reach.**

I know that together, we can bring Pennsylvania and our country the leadership we deserve.

**[Will you chip in $5 now to help us get to our most important goal so far this election? We cannot afford to fall short now.](https://act.myngp.com/el/-386059307268964352/-4191984837919244288?refcode=e20160314&refcode2=e20160314nd&amount=25)**

Thanks for being a part of this,

Katie

Every fundraising goal is a must-hit if we want to end Pat Toomey's failed leadership and bring Pennsylvania values to the U.S. Senate. Pennsylvania can't afford six more years of Toomey -- to say nothing of a Republican Senate majority combined with a Donald Trump presidency. But that nightmare has a real chance of coming true if each and every one of us doesn't give this election our all. There are still so many undecided voters out there who we need to reach. I know that together, we can bring Pennsylvania and our country the leadership we deserve. Thanks for being a part of this, Katie | | | Paid For By Katie McGinty for Senate

body:

And there's one thing that we can all do when it's this close. Let's reach for our goal of 150,000 contributions by midnight tonight and show Bernie we have his back.

With Ohio, Illinois, Missouri, Florida, and North Carolina all voting tomorrow, the results from these races mean we have a chance to keep up our momentum.

In solidarity,
Jeff Weaver

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: I had a choice
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bernie 2016
body:
Sisters and Brothers -
When I decided to run for president, I had to decide: should I start a super PAC and travel the country begging millionaires and billionaires to contribute to my campaign? It took me about a tenth of a second to decide against that.
Instead, we chose a different path, and it's very moving to me that our campaign has received more than 5 million individual contributions from working people and the middle class who want us to transform this country in significant ways.
So that's why I send these emails, because it is how we're able to run a campaign that is not beholden to Wall Street, the pharmaceutical industry, and those who profit from the destruction of the planet. And it's why I have to ask directly - one more time - ahead of the most important day of the campaign to date:
[**Can I count on you to contribute $3 to our campaign to help us hit our goal of 150,000 individual contributions before midnight? We 're very close, and yours could be the one that puts us over.**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/a-moment-of-truth?refcode=em160314pm3-full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/a-moment-of-truth?refcode=em160314pm3-full")
This campaign is about a grassroots movement of Americans standing up and saying: "Enough is enough. This country and our government belong to all of us, not just a handful of billionaires."
Every single contribution we receive not only amplifies that message, but helps ensure we'll have the resources we need to win.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders
